page: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 3 physical cores and 6 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with hyper-threading enabled. A key highlight of this server is its massive memory capacity of 504.0 GB, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per physical core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 1417 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX24-6mds_v2 demonstrates strong single-core performance. It achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2362.0 and a text processing score of 2948.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded compression tasks are also highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 34.83 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.37 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 6660.14 and Geekbench multi-core score of 7259.0, align with average tier results. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is recorded at 51.2 GB/sec, while write bandwidth reaches 36.19 GB/sec.

Given its massive memory footprint relative to its 6 vCPUs, this instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is highly suitable for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and heavy text-processing pipelines. However, a clear tradeoff is visible in compute-intensive parallel tasks; for instance, while small LLM inference text generation is strong at 258.94 tokens/sec, larger models like the 7B parameter model drop to a poor 4.59 tokens/sec due to the limited physical core count.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it provides 3 physical cores and 6 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. The server stands out with an extraordinary 504.0 GB of RAM, translating to 84.0 GB per core, alongside 1417 GB of local storage. It lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2362.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 34.83 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is moderate, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 7259.0. This resource profile makes the FX24-6mds_v2 highly cost-efficient for large in-memory databases, caching systems, and text-processing applications, though it presents a performance tradeoff for highly parallelized compute tasks or large-scale AI model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, 6 vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and 1417 GB of local storage, designed for large in-memory database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 6 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an extraordinary memory-to-core ratio and high-speed single-core performance for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 3 physical cores and 6 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
Massive 504.0 GB RAM capacity providing 84.0 GB of memory per core
1417 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text-processing benchmarks
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text-processing pipelines
